cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out which of the two options is sarcastic. Let's look at each one. Option A: "One of the worst things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." This statement is straightforward. It says that pushing the trolley was one of the worst things to do. There's no obvious sarcasm here; it's a direct statement of dislike. Option B: "One of the enjoyable things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." This statement says that pushing the trolley was an enjoyable activity. Now, pushing returns around a store is typically not seen as an enjoyable task in retail.
